summary: Prepend the finalized shared rubric (.framework/plan21/meta/rubric.final.md) verbatim to every prompt. This is the single highest-leverage change: every good-fixture over-flag in the baseline traces to an agent not inheriting these clauses (severity ladder, codebase-bar incl. auto-instrumentation + hardening-is-info carve-outs, internal-consistency, scope discipline, grounding/diff-awareness, JSON-only output).
prompt_rewrite: Prepend rubric.final.md verbatim above each agent's domain-specific body. Then add the per-agent scope carve-outs and clauses listed in each agent's entry below.

Rewrite: enumerate what the framework auto-provides (auto correlation_id/trace via structlog processors + FastAPIInstrumentor span + ObservabilityMiddleware request log/metrics) and instruct NOT to flag its absence. Remove the three template-contradicting flag clauses: (a) missing manual span/metric/log on an auto-instrumented route, (b) missing correlation_id on error paths (auto-injected), (c) missing per-endpoint SLO (template ships fleet-wide SLOs only). Reserve high for a genuine in-domain gap: a mutation/business path with NO structured log of its own, a path that BYPASSES the auto-instrumented seam, or active error suppression. Add scope carve-out: do NOT flag DB transaction/rollback/session state (data-integrity owns it) or idempotency/business correctness (application-logic owns it).

fixture_edits.detail: The one in-domain defect (no structured log on the mutation path) is buried under cross-domain bait (commit-failure, double-prefix idempotency, inline HTTPException import). Strip the bait; pin expect.json to the structured-logging-absence dimension.
Genuinely clean (more instrumented than shipped get_items); should score [] once prompt is fixed.
Genuinely clean; all 5 baseline findings are below the codebase bar.

notes: Keystone of the over-flag cohort: its three flag clauses each contradict the shipped template. Already has 2 good fixtures, so no additional good fixture needed. Audit-proposed start recall_min 0.50 / fp_max 0.10; re-derive Phase 3.

prompt_rewrite: Rewrite the trigger: it currently describes the codebase's default-and-only idiom (auto-instrumented engine), so it would flag every query in the repo. Re-scope to instrumentation-BYPASS: a non-instrumented store/path (raw DBAPI call, second engine not registered with SQLAlchemyInstrumentor, background path), a new DB client with no /health, or a data-layer error swallowed without correlation_id. Defer pagination/unbounded-query to performance.

fixture_edits.detail: Byte-identical body to the good fixture; auto-instrumented + bounded (a non-defect). Replace with raw-connection-bypasses-instrumentation (a second un-registered engine / raw DBAPI call).
The delete is auto-instrumented like create_item; the 'invisible to observability' docstring is factually false. Replace with new-client-no-health OR db-error-no-correlation-id.

notes: Coupled prompt+fixture redesign. Both bad fixtures are non-defects -> recall structurally 0. Do NOT set a block_threshold until the redesign produces a true-positive signal (Phase 3). good fixture is fine; no second needed.

prompt_rewrite: Re-key bullets 1+2 from 'view with no RUM' (the global window error/unhandledrejection handler already covers views) to ACTIVE error suppression: a catch that stops the rejection reaching the global handler / js-errors counter, or an error boundary returning null. Narrow bullet 4 (beacon allowlist) to the unbounded-label-cardinality core; cross-reference privacy for the PII angle (privacy owns beacon PII).

fixture_edits.detail: The global window handler covers the unhandled rejection exactly like App.tsx, so 0/3 'miss' is correct reviewer behavior. Re-author into an ACTIVE-suppression variant (.catch(()=>{}) / error boundary returning null).

INVALID-GOOD: device_class is stripped server-side by the fail-closed allowlist (dead-on-arrival), so reviewers correctly flag it. Add device_class to the allowlist in-patch OR use an allowlisted/server-derived dimension.

notes: Good fixture is being re-authored to genuine-clean, not added to. medium 'no alert/dashboard for new signal' findings are valid but advisory; do not gate. recall_min 0.90 / fp_max 0.10.

prompt_rewrite: Add a diff-awareness clause: files added in THIS diff are present; only flag a missing alert/dashboard when absent from BOTH diff and tree (an r1 repeat hallucinated a high for an alert the patch ADDS). Pin severities deterministically: PRIMARY wiring break (exporter with no scrape / scrape with no exporter / dev-only obs that never reaches prod / new prod surface with no obs) = HIGH (blocks); SECONDARY parity gap (surface scraped but no alert, or no dashboard) = MEDIUM (stable, no block). Soften the per-alert-receiver clause (template uses a catch-all route).

fixture_edits.detail: NOT-CLEAN: the dev.yml redis block is indented under volumes: not services: (parses as a malformed volume), which r0/r2 correctly flag. Repair the placement under services:.

notes: Repairs cross-repeat severity wobble (high/medium on the same satellite gap) and the diff-awareness hallucination. Good fixture is repaired, not added to.

prompt_rewrite: Rewrite to inherit the rubric and NARROW the domain to AUDIT-GAP + RETENTION-PATH + ERASURE-PATH only. Defer ALL raw-PII handling to privacy (currently 2 of 3 bad-fixture findings per repeat are privacy's domain). Add codebase-bar clause: do NOT flag structured get_logger().info() of an OPAQUE identifier or absence of a per-line retention policy (the template's own observability middleware and webhook inbox log this way). High is reserved for: a sensitive state-changing op with NO audit record, or persisted personal data with NO retention/deletion path anywhere reachable.

fixture_edits.detail: Valid defect but mis-targeted: its loud defect is raw-email-in-logs (privacy's domain) while the compliance defect (audit gap) is buried. Re-author to a true compliance defect (sensitive-op-with-no-audit-record OR persisted-PII-with-no-deletion-path); move the email-logging case to the privacy corpus.
Genuinely clean (opaque-id structured log). The prompt is what must change to stop flagging it.
Add a 2nd clean good fixture (e.g. a sensitive op that DOES write an audit record + has a deletion path) to widen fp coverage on this fp-1.00 agent.

notes: fp 1.00 on a single good fixture -> borderline-on-precision; add a 2nd good fixture to widen fp coverage. Treat high as ADVISORY (non-blocking) until the rewrite + re-author drop fp from 1.00; re-derive recall_min 0.90 / fp_max 0.10 in Phase 3.

prompt_rewrite: Rewrite to inherit the rubric; replace the unbounded 'at least high' floor with a demonstrability gate (high requires a concrete demonstrable data-loss/atomicity defect). Add codebase-bar note: the session factory sets expire_on_commit=False and created_at uses server_default, so a missing post-commit refresh is NOT a defect (do not hallucinate 'created_at=None'); items.name has no UNIQUE constraint, so absence of de-dup is not a violated invariant. High stays for the genuine planted defect (the in-loop session.commit() that breaks batch atomicity).

fixture_edits.detail: Clean as a data-integrity matter, but its docstring 'no per-row refresh' hands the agent the false created_at=None FP. Align the docstring to the template idiom (note expire_on_commit=False) so it stops baiting the over-flag.
Add a 2nd clean good fixture (a different atomic write path) to widen fp coverage on this fp-1.00 agent.

notes: fp 1.00 on a single good fixture -> borderline-on-precision; add a 2nd good fixture. Expect fp to drop to ~0-0.33 after the codebase-bar clause + good-fixture alignment.

prompt_rewrite: Prepend rubric; add a clause binding 'secret-exposing' to an ACTUAL committed/leaked secret VALUE on a changed line (hardcoded default, key in code/config, secret in a logged field). Forbid hardening-as-high/medium: SecretStr / min_length / rotation / .env handling are info at most (template uses plain str signing secrets; .env is already gitignored). Enforce added/modified-lines-only review (stop reaching into lru_cache/env_file/model_dump out-of-diff). Add scope carve-out: field naming is code-quality/usability, not security.

fixture_edits.detail: GENUINELY CLEAN (api_secret_key: str matches template webhook_signing_secret: str). The over-flag is 100% prompt defect; no change to change.patch needed. OPTIONAL: re-theme to mirror the HMAC idiom for representativeness.
Add a 2nd clean good fixture (e.g. an env-sourced secret consumed via get_secret-style accessor) to widen fp coverage on this fp-1.00 agent.

notes: fp 1.00 (2-3 findings on the good fixture every run) + severity instability (SecretStr high in r1, medium in r0/r2) -> borderline-on-precision; add a 2nd good fixture. The load-bearing fix is the prompt clause forbidding hardening-as-high; a threshold change alone will not fix r1's escalation. bad fixture (hardcoded default) is a clean discriminating critical.

prompt_rewrite: Anchor redundant/duplicate-coverage explicitly to LOW/INFO (advisory, never blocks). Reserve high STRICTLY for 'a test that cannot fail' (tautology, mock of the symbol-under-test, interface-mismatch mock, unhappy-path-without-failure-assert). Add codebase-bar note: per-field env-override tests match the existing suite idiom.

fixture_edits.detail: INVALID: test_resolved_log_level_prod_is_info is a VERBATIM duplicate of an existing parametrized [prod] case (visible via the bundle context), which reviewers correctly flag. Replace the duplicate with a genuinely additive distinct-behavior test.
Add a 2nd clean good fixture (a meaningful additive test) to widen fp coverage on this fp-1.00 agent.

notes: fp 1.00 driven by the verbatim-duplicate test + an unanchored prompt that rated the SAME duplicate high/low/medium across repeats. Coupled fixture+prompt fix; add a 2nd good fixture to widen coverage. Recall (a-test-that-cannot-fail) is perfect and stable.

prompt_rewrite: Add a container-reach clause: a var wired only into a dev/test overlay (base.yml) but needed in prod is a real parity gap when prod.yml/staging.yml have no env_file passthrough. Resolve the container-reach seam deterministically. Add grounding clause (an r2 repeat fabricated a .env.example list from memory). Scope carve-out: config VALUE divergence / observability / PII content are other agents' domains.

fixture_edits.detail: INVALID: the base.yml-only var wiring IS a genuine staging/prod parity gap; the compose hunk is byte-identical to the bad fixture, distinguished only by a parity-irrelevant .env.example line. Rewrite to a pure consumed+declared env-var change with NO base.yml hunk.
Add a 2nd clean good fixture (another parity-complete env-var change) to widen fp coverage given the current 0.67.

notes: fp 0.67 from one mis-authored good fixture on an unresolved container-reach seam (recall is a clean 1.00) -> borderline-on-precision; add a 2nd good fixture. Do NOT widen fp_max to absorb 0.67; fix the fixture+prompt. Keep recall_min 0.90 / fp_max 0.10.

prompt_rewrite: Prepend rubric; add a bounded-reads codebase-bar clause: bounded reads (capped by MAX_PAGE_SIZE) that hydrate full ORM rows match the repo idiom (the template's own list_items does select(Item) and discards created_at unflagged) and are NOT defects at any severity. Keep high for unbounded scans / N+1 / pool exhaustion / super-linear-on-unbounded-input (all correctly rated today).

fixture_edits.detail: Make the good fixture unambiguously clean so it yields [] (its get_item_names reuses list_items and discards a hydrated column, the exact codebase-bar/internal-consistency pattern the bad fixture's own suggested projection fix penalizes).
Add a 2nd clean good fixture (a bounded query matching the repo idiom) to widen fp coverage; the single good fixture currently draws a low-severity codebase-bar over-flag on all 3 repeats.

notes: Good fixture over-flags (low) on all 3 repeats via a codebase-bar violation -> borderline-on-precision; add a 2nd good fixture. At block=high the good fixture already passes (low does not block); target is a literal [] via the codebase-bar clause. Bad-fixture detection (N+1 + unbounded scan, high 3/3) is excellent.

prompt_rewrite: Prepend rubric (replace the ad-hoc flat rule; outcome unchanged — PII logged/echoed/captured-into-telemetry stays high). Add an explicit scope line: personal-data only; defer authz->security, validation/correctness->application-logic, contract->contracts, observability coverage->observability. privacy is the SOLE owner of whether personal data leaks; other agents cross-reference but never re-flag.

fixture_edits.detail: De-compound to a single response-leak so the expected dimension is unambiguous.
Add a 2nd good fixture (pii-redacted-log: an opaque-id structured log that is correctly clean) to widen fp coverage; only 1 good case exists today on this high-stakes sole-owner agent.

notes: Cleanest agent in the corpus (recall 1.00 / fp 0.00) but only ONE good fixture. Borderline-on-precision-COVERAGE rather than borderline-on-fp: a single good case gives weak fp signal for the agent that owns every PII boundary, so add a 2nd good fixture (synthesis-recommended). recall_min 0.90 / fp_max 0.10.

prompt_rewrite: KEEP the prompt (scope-disciplined, fp 0.00; the reviewers' info classifications for stale-pact / optional-undeclared-read are CORRECT). Prepend rubric and add a hard-vs-optional-dependency clarifier: a HARD undeclared dependency (int(data['field']) -> KeyError) is high; an OPTIONAL undeclared read (data.get(...)) or a not-yet-regenerated pact is info, never blocking. The recall 0.50 FAIL is a FIXTURE-grading bug, not a prompt or threshold problem.

fixture_edits.detail: INVALID as a high-graded bad: exercises only info-tier behavior (stale pact + optional .get() read) but is scored against block_threshold=high. Convert the optional read into a HARD undeclared dependency (int(data['field']) -> KeyError with no pact declaration) so it becomes a genuine high.
MISSING: the prompt's third high category (weakened consumer assertion) is untested. Add a bad fixture for it.

notes: Recall 0.50 fix is entirely in the FIXTURE, not the threshold or a 2nd good fixture. fp is a clean 0.00, so no precision concern -> no second good fixture.

prompt_rewrite: KEEP the prompt's correct high bar (PII to undocumented store / deletion gap missing a store / stale documented-or-indexed derived field). Prepend rubric and add a normalization codebase-bar carve-out (the template's create_item stores name verbatim, unflagged; absence of strip/lower normalization is NOT a lineage concern). Add a derived-field-desync class pinned high. The recall 0.17 FAIL is a FIXTURE defect; the reviewers correctly abstained.

fixture_edits.detail: INVALID: demands flagging verbatim-name storage the template's create_item does unflagged (codebase-bar) AND is out of domain (no PII sink, no deletion gap). Sole cause of recall 0.17. Replace with deletion-gap-misses-a-store OR PII-to-undocumented-sink.

notes: Recall 0.17 fix is replacing one invalid bad fixture; fp is a clean 0.00 -> no precision concern, no second good fixture. Raise recall_min to 0.90 after the replacement.

prompt_rewrite: Rewrite to inherit the rubric; cap output at low/info (advisory, never high/medium). Add a scope fence: docstrings (when siblings document) / .env.example vars / README-or-spec staleness / complex-logic comments only — disclaim correctness, response_model/typing, openapi.json regen, page-size-cap, and CI-contract jobs (those belong to contracts/api-design/performance). Pin the doc-completeness finding at a single severity (low). Add grounding clause (cite the changed line).

fixture_edits.detail: NOT CLEAN: count_items uses list_items (bounded to DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE) while the docstring promises a true total, which reviewers correctly flag as a doc-vs-impl mismatch. Re-author to a true aggregate OR an honest bounded docstring.

notes: Advisory agent (fp_max 1.00 by design). fp 1.00 is surfacing-by-design, not a real false positive; fix the good fixture's genuine doc-vs-impl mismatch rather than add a 2nd good fixture. recall_min 0.90 / fp_max 1.00.

prompt_rewrite: Add a scope-discipline clause: you see ONLY the manifest diff, not call sites — do NOT reason about runtime/async/event-loop behavior or invent httpx.AsyncClient refactors. Add a no-fabrication clause: no unverifiable CVE ids; phrase pin-floor concerns generically. Explicitly opt out of the medium tier (cap at low even for a convention violation); keep a single narrow optional high ONLY for a concrete supply-chain compromise (malicious/typosquat/yanked), advisory until a fixture proves high-precision detection.

fixture_edits.detail: NOT-UNAMBIGUOUSLY-CLEAN: httpx>=0.27,<1.0 diverges below the template >=0.28 floor and adds a non-template upper cap; correctly flagged. Change to httpx>=0.28 and drop the cap.

notes: Advisory agent (fp_max 1.00). fp 1.00 is surfacing-by-design; fix the good fixture's actual pin divergence rather than add a 2nd good fixture. recall_min 0.90 / fp_max 1.00.

prompt_rewrite: Prompt: add a scope exclusion (a new component not yet wired into a parent is NOT a usability defect — integration completeness is no agent's concern). Allow low for concrete defects (silent mutation failure, no error branch) while keeping info for taste. Harden JSON-only output (output exactly [] when nothing to flag). The fp 0.33 FAIL is a CALIBRATION artifact, not a prompt defect.

fixture_edits.detail: Valid defect, but change.patch lacks the diff --git/index header -> inconsistent line anchoring across repeats. Regenerate as a full git-formatted diff; do NOT change the defect.

notes: fp 0.33 is purely a thresholds.yaml mismatch (advisory agent held to a blocking fp_max 0.10), NOT borderline-on-precision -> no second good fixture. The fix is the calibration change (see usability-calibration-001), not the fixture or prompt. recall_min 0.90 / fp_max 1.00.

prompt_rewrite: Prepend rubric; add an explicit CORRECTNESS-only domain line and a high/medium calibration line (a defect real TODAY = high; a latent fragility relying on accidental correctness = medium). Add codebase-bar note: the template uses both `if not X` and `if X is None` legitimately — do not flag the codebase's own falsy guards. Scope carve-out: defer contract/schema/api-design/security/data-integrity concerns.

fixture_edits.detail: WEAK/BORDERLINE + factually-WRONG inline comment ('returns 404 for any id=0' is false — item is the ORM object, not item_id). Fix the comment to state the real latent-ORM-truthiness defect; optionally upgrade to an unambiguous present-tense defect.

notes: PASS today (recall 1.00 / fp 0.00). No precision concern -> no second good fixture. Treat medium as advisory until a true-high bad fixture exists.

prompt_rewrite: Prepend rubric; add a scope clause excluding import-hygiene/dead-code/naming/style (code-quality owns those). Add a secondary-finding-folding clause: report one root layering defect ONCE at high and fold in-domain secondary symptoms into it rather than double-counting as independent blockers.

fixture_edits.detail: Valid + clean. Add a good/lightweight-inline-handler good fixture to pin the heavy-vs-lightweight inline-handler carve-out (the prompt's untested second domain).
Add to cover the prompt's untested second domain alongside the new lightweight good fixture.
Valid multi-file fixture — the documented Phase-0a hunk-counter FALSE POSITIVE. Do NOT regenerate or 'fix' it.

notes: PASS today (recall 1.00 / fp 0.00). The good-fixture additions are coverage for the inline-handler domain, NOT a precision fix -> needs_second_good_fixture=false (the additions are coverage-driven, tracked under fixture_edits, not the precision signal).

prompt_rewrite: Prepend rubric; re-map the two flag-as-info categories (nullability, mutation-design) to LOW (advisory) so 'flag' always carries an actionable severity; never block on them. Add a codebase-bar carve-out for the bare-payload/non-null template idiom. Scope: GraphQL (Strawberry, code-first) schema only — defer REST/OpenAPI parity to contracts.

fixture_edits.detail: Valid defect (breaking field rename) but the name implies a REST-parity responsibility the prompt does not own. Rename to graphql-breaking-field-rename.
WEAK/DEGENERATE: REST-only, tests only scope-obedience not GraphQL judgment. Add a genuine GraphQL additive good fixture (graphql-additive-field: an additive optional GraphQL field returns []).

notes: PASS today (recall 1.00 / fp 0.00). The added GraphQL good fixture is coverage of the real over-flag boundary, not a fp-precision rescue -> needs_second_good_fixture=false (coverage-driven add, tracked under fixture_edits).

prompt_rewrite: Prepend rubric; allow stepping a single non-essential-control finding down to medium. Otherwise unchanged.

fixture_edits.detail: Add a good+bad pair for the div-onClick-as-button category (untested high category).
Add a good+bad pair for keyboard-inaccessibility (untested high category). Only img-alt is currently exercised.

notes: Healthy (bad_recall 1.00 / good_fp 0.00). The added good fixtures are coverage for two untested high categories, not a precision rescue -> needs_second_good_fixture=false (coverage-driven add, tracked under fixture_edits).

summary: Realign tests/eval/fixtures/thresholds.yaml usability fp_max from 0.10 -> 1.00 to match block_threshold=None advisory semantics (mirroring dependency/documentation). usability's baseline fp 0.33 'FAIL' is entirely this threshold-semantics mismatch: an advisory agent (engine treats block_threshold=None) scored at a blocking fp_max. This is the ONLY genuine threshold bug in the corpus.
prompt_rewrite: (none — see usability-001 for the prompt clauses)

summary: Do NOT widen any fp_max to 'absorb' a current baseline over-flag — every elevated fp in this baseline is a prompt or fixture defect, not real over-flagging; widening would mask future regressions. After all coupled prompt+fixture edits land, re-derive every numeric recall_min/fp_max via `framework eval-analyze` on a post-fix --repeat sweep (Phase 3). observability-db's threshold is deferred entirely until its coupled prompt+fixture redesign produces a true-positive signal.
prompt_rewrite: (none)
block_threshold: see per-agent entries (high for all blocking agents; null/none for dependency, documentation, usability; deferred for observability-db)
